title: Code Execution, Computer Use, Sandboxes, Prompt Injection, and Artifacts · Python Build Room
summary: A public-interest product team incrementally builds deny-by-default disposable action sandbox from original fixed provider, interaction, tool, approval, evaluation, and failure fixtures while retaining prior Python, HTTP, prompt, loop, security, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of deny-by-default disposable action sandbox while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Classify generated code as untrusted and define supported language, runtime, packages, inputs, outputs, time, memory, process, file, network, and secret boundaries.
Run code in a disposable isolated environment with deny-by-default capabilities, immutable base, bounded resources, no host mounts, and complete destruction.
Mediate files, environment, network destinations, DNS, uploads, downloads, subprocesses, and credentials through typed policy and explicit approvals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Code Execution Risk
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop agent engineering team handles case 7156 during an accessible public-service intake assistant. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate code and computer-use actions through disposable sandboxes, deny-by-default capabilities, injection-resistant previews, approvals, and artifact inspection; every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy; then introduce a sensitive action, poisoned memory, leaking log, or computer-use request and deny or pause it. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify generated code as untrusted and define supported language, runtime, packages, inputs, outputs, time, memory, process, file, network, and secret boundaries.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Code Execution Risk.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for classify generated code as untrusted and define supported language, runtime, packages, inputs, outputs, time, memory, process, file, network, and secret boundaries. For Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Code Execution Risk, keep Code Execution Risk connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Artifact Inspection
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-artifact-inspection-ri871h" that uses case 8784 to gate code and computer-use actions through disposable sandboxes, deny-by-default capabilities, injection-resistant previews, approvals, and artifact inspection. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Artifact Inspection.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Artifact Inspection into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Artifact Inspection, keep Artifact Inspection connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-artifact-inspection-ri871h
# Competency: agentpy-artifact-inspection.
# Case 8784: a privacy-minimized nonprofit workflow.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: inject cancellation, retry, repeated-call, checkpoint, or replay failure and recover without duplicate effects.
def evidence_agentpy_artifact_inspection_ri871h(requested_actions: int = 30, sandboxed_actions: int = 12, denied_actions: int = 12, approval_actions: int = 6, host_escapes: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ri871h = "ri871h-h178ir-8784"
    assert evidence_variant_ri871h
    evidence_axes_ri871h = (
        "authorityri871h",
        "interaction8784",
        "toolh178ir",
        "stateri871h8784",
        "evaluation8784ri871h",
        "recoveryh178ir8784",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ri871h)) == 6
    if requested_actions < 1 or sandboxed_actions < 0 or denied_actions < 0:
        return False, requested_actions, "sandbox-action-count"
    if sandboxed_actions + denied_actions + approval_actions != requested_actions:
        return False, sandboxed_actions, "sandbox-reconciliation"
    if approval_actions < 2:
        return False, approval_actions, "computer-use-approval"
    if host_escapes > 0:
        return False, host_escapes, "sandbox-escape"
    assert sandboxed_actions + denied_actions + approval_actions == requested_actions and host_escapes == 0
    return True, sandboxed_actions, "contained-agent-actions"
